available_tools.description: Comprehensive documentation of available MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools for SlideHeroes development workflows. These tools provide integration with external services, database management, observability, and enhanced development capabilities.

tool.name: newrelic_mcp
tool.summary: New Relic observability and monitoring integration for application performance insights
tool.description: The New Relic MCP Server provides comprehensive observability capabilities including log queries, transaction traces, error analysis, and OpenTelemetry trace inspection. Essential for monitoring and debugging the SlideHeroes application in production and development environments.

capabilities.capability: Query application logs using NRQL (New Relic Query Language)
Inspect error traces and stack traces
Debug production issues with real-time data

tool_function.name: query_newrelic_logs
tool_function.description: Query New Relic logs using NRQL for powerful log analysis

example.query: SELECT * FROM Log WHERE service.name = 'slideheroes-web' SINCE 1 hour ago

tool_function.name: get_transaction_traces
tool_function.description: Get recent transaction traces from New Relic

tool_function.name: get_error_traces
tool_function.description: Get recent error traces from New Relic

parameters.parameter: Number of error traces to return

tool_function.name: get_otel_traces
tool_function.description: Get OpenTelemetry traces from New Relic

best_practices.practice: Use NRQL queries to filter logs by service name, error level, or custom attributes
Correlate error traces with transaction traces to understand failure context
Use OpenTelemetry traces for distributed tracing across microservices
Set appropriate timeouts for complex NRQL queries
Filter by app_name or service_name to reduce noise in results

tool.name: postgres_mcp
tool.summary: Local PostgreSQL database management for Supabase local development
tool.description: The PostgreSQL MCP Server provides extensive database management capabilities for your LOCAL Supabase instance. Use this tool when working with your local development database (typically running on localhost:54322). Includes schema operations, query execution, performance analysis, security management, and database administration. For production/remote Supabase operations, use the Supabase MCP tool instead.

capabilities.capability: Execute SQL queries with parameterized statements
Manage functions, triggers, and stored procedures
Analyze query performance and database configuration
Add and manage database comments for documentation

tool_function.name: pg_execute_query
tool_function.description: Execute SELECT queries and data retrieval operations

parameters.parameter: Query operation: select, count, or exists

tool_function.name: pg_execute_mutation
tool_function.description: Execute data modification operations (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/UPSERT)

parameters.parameter: Mutation operation: insert, update, delete, or upsert

tool_function.name: pg_execute_sql
tool_function.description: Execute arbitrary SQL statements including DDL operations

tool_function.name: pg_manage_schema
tool_function.description: Manage PostgreSQL schema - get info, create/alter tables, manage enums

parameters.parameter: Operation: get_info, create_table, alter_table, get_enums, create_enum

tool_function.name: pg_manage_indexes
tool_function.description: Manage PostgreSQL indexes - get, create, drop, reindex, analyze usage

parameters.parameter: Operation: get, create, drop, reindex, analyze_usage

tool_function.name: pg_manage_rls
tool_function.description: Manage PostgreSQL Row-Level Security - enable/disable RLS and manage policies

parameters.parameter: Operation: enable, disable, create_policy, drop_policy, get_policies

tool_function.name: pg_manage_users
tool_function.description: Manage PostgreSQL users and permissions

parameters.parameter: Operation: create, drop, alter, grant, revoke, get_permissions, list

best_practices.practice: Use this tool for LOCAL development only (localhost:54322)
Always use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
Use pg_manage_rls to implement Row-Level Security for multi-tenant data
Run pg_analyze_database periodically to check performance and security
Use transactions for complex multi-step operations
Document database objects using pg_manage_comments
Test migrations locally before applying to remote via Supabase MCP

tool.name: supabase_mcp
tool.summary: Remote Supabase platform management for production and staging
tool.description: The Supabase MCP Server provides comprehensive platform management capabilities for REMOTE Supabase instances (production, staging, and cloud-hosted projects). Use this tool for managing cloud-hosted Supabase projects, including database operations, edge functions deployment, branching workflows, and monitoring. For local development database operations, use the PostgreSQL MCP tool instead.

capabilities.capability: Generate TypeScript types from database schema

tool_function.name: list_projects
tool_function.description: Lists all Supabase projects for the user

tool_function.name: create_project
tool_function.description: Creates a new Supabase project

tool_function.name: apply_migration
tool_function.description: Applies a migration to the database (use for DDL operations)

tool_function.name: execute_sql
tool_function.description: Executes raw SQL in the Postgres database

tool_function.name: generate_typescript_types
tool_function.description: Generates TypeScript types for a project

tool_function.name: deploy_edge_function
tool_function.description: Deploys an Edge Function to a Supabase project

tool_function.name: get_logs
tool_function.description: Gets logs for a Supabase project by service type

parameters.parameter: Service type: api, postgres, edge-function, auth, storage, realtime

tool_function.name: get_advisors
tool_function.description: Gets advisory notices for security and performance

best_practices.practice: Use this tool for REMOTE/CLOUD Supabase instances only
Always use apply_migration for DDL operations instead of execute_sql
Run get_advisors regularly to check for security vulnerabilities
Use development branches for testing before merging to production
Generate TypeScript types after schema changes
Monitor logs when debugging edge function issues
Test locally with PostgreSQL MCP before deploying to remote

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.summary: GitHub API integration for repository and code management
tool.description: The GitHub MCP Server provides seamless integration with GitHub APIs, enabling advanced automation and interaction capabilities. Supports full CRUD operations on repositories, issues, pull requests, and comprehensive code management.

capabilities.capability: File operations (create, update, read, push multiple files)
Pull request operations (create, merge, review, comment)
Search across repositories, code, issues, and users

tool_function.name: create_or_update_file
tool_function.description: Create or update a single file in a repository

tool_function.name: push_files
tool_function.description: Push multiple files in a single commit

parameters.parameter: Array of files with path and content

tool_function.name: create_branch
tool_function.description: Create a new branch in a GitHub repository

tool_function.name: create_pull_request
tool_function.description: Create a new pull request

tool_function.name: create_pull_request_review
tool_function.description: Create a review on a pull request

parameters.parameter: Review action: APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or COMMENT

best_practices.practice: Use push_files for batch operations instead of multiple create_or_update_file calls
Always create a branch before making changes to avoid direct commits to main
Use draft PRs for work in progress to signal incomplete changes
Include detailed commit messages and PR descriptions
Search for existing issues/PRs before creating duplicates

tool.name: cloudflare_mcp
tool.summary: Cloudflare platform management for Workers, R2, D1, and other services
tool.description: The Cloudflare MCP provides comprehensive management capabilities for Cloudflare services including Workers, KV namespaces, R2 buckets, D1 databases, and Hyperdrive configurations. Also includes documentation search and observability features.

capabilities.capability: Manage Workers (list, get details, get source code)
KV namespace operations (create, list, update, delete)
R2 bucket management (create, list, get, delete)
D1 database operations (create, query, list, delete)

tool_function.name: workers_list
tool_function.description: List all Workers in your Cloudflare account

tool_function.name: workers_get_worker_code
tool_function.description: Get the source code of a Cloudflare Worker

tool_function.name: query_worker_observability
tool_function.description: Query Workers logs and metrics using observability API

parameters.parameter: Query object with view, parameters, and timeframe

tool_function.name: kv_namespace_create
tool_function.description: Create a new KV namespace

tool_function.name: r2_bucket_create
tool_function.description: Create a new R2 bucket

tool_function.name: d1_database_create
tool_function.description: Create a new D1 database

tool_function.name: d1_database_query
tool_function.description: Query a D1 database

best_practices.practice: Use observability_keys before applying filters in worker queries
Set active account before performing operations that require accountId
Use the migration guide when moving from Pages to Workers
Query worker observability data for debugging and performance analysis

tool.name: browser_tools_mcp
tool.summary: Browser automation and testing tools for web application development
tool.description: Browser tools MCP provides automated browser interaction capabilities including navigation, element interaction, screenshot capture, and comprehensive auditing for accessibility, performance, SEO, and best practices.

capabilities.capability: Element clicking, typing, and form interaction

tool_function.name: takeScreenshot
tool_function.description: Take a screenshot of the current browser tab

tool_function.name: runAccessibilityAudit
tool_function.description: Run an accessibility audit on the current page

tool_function.name: runPerformanceAudit
tool_function.description: Run a performance audit on the current page

tool_function.name: runSEOAudit
tool_function.description: Run an SEO audit on the current page

tool_function.name: getConsoleErrors
tool_function.description: Check browser console errors

best_practices.practice: Run accessibility audits to ensure WCAG compliance
Use performance audits to identify optimization opportunities
Check console errors during development and debugging
Take screenshots for visual regression testing
Run SEO audits to improve search engine visibility

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.summary: Real-time documentation retrieval for libraries and frameworks
tool.description: Context7 MCP provides up-to-date, version-specific documentation and code examples directly from authoritative sources. Eliminates reliance on potentially outdated training data.

capabilities.capability: Fetch current documentation for any library or framework
Resolve library names to Context7-compatible IDs

tool_function.name: resolve-library-id
tool_function.description: Resolves a library name to a Context7-compatible ID

tool_function.name: get-library-docs
tool_function.description: Fetches documentation for a library

best_practices.practice: Always resolve library names to IDs before fetching docs
Use topic parameter to get focused documentation
Request documentation for the specific version you're using
Use higher token counts for comprehensive documentation

tool.name: perplexity_mcp
tool.summary: AI-powered conversational search and information retrieval
tool.description: Perplexity MCP enables conversational AI interactions using the Sonar API for enhanced information retrieval and question answering capabilities.

capabilities.capability: Access current information through AI search

tool_function.name: perplexity_ask
tool_function.description: Engages in conversation using the Sonar API

parameters.parameter: Array of conversation messages with role and content

tool.name: exa_mcp
tool.summary: Advanced web search with neural and keyword capabilities
tool.description: Exa MCP provides sophisticated web search capabilities with both neural (semantic) and keyword-based search options, domain filtering, and content categorization.

capabilities.capability: Filter by content categories (news, research, GitHub, etc.)

tool_function.name: exa_search
tool_function.description: Search the web using Exa API

parameters.parameter: Search type: auto, neural, or keyword

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.summary: Sequential thinking tool for complex problem-solving
tool.description: Code reasoning MCP provides structured thinking capabilities for breaking down complex problems, revising thoughts, and exploring alternative solution paths.

capabilities.capability: Break down complex problems into manageable steps
Revise and refine thoughts as understanding deepens
Maintain context over multiple thinking steps

tool_function.name: code-reasoning
tool_function.description: Dynamic problem-solving through sequential thinking

guideline.title: Tool Combination Strategies
guideline.description: These tools work synergistically to create powerful development workflows: - Use Context7 for up-to-date documentation before implementing features - Use Code Reasoning to break down complex requirements - Use GitHub MCP for version control and collaboration - Use Supabase/Postgres MCP for database operations - Use Browser Tools for testing and validation - Use New Relic for monitoring and debugging - Use Cloudflare tools for edge computing and CDN management

guideline.title: SlideHeroes Development Workflow
guideline.description: Typical SlideHeroes development pattern: 1. Use Code Reasoning to analyze feature requirements 2. Use Context7 to get current framework and library documentation 3. Use PostgreSQL MCP for LOCAL database schema changes and testing 4. Use GitHub MCP to create feature branches and commits 5. Use Browser Tools to test accessibility and performance 6. Use Supabase MCP to deploy changes to REMOTE/PRODUCTION 7. Use New Relic to monitor application behavior 8. Use Cloudflare for edge function deployment if needed

guideline.title: Debugging and Monitoring
guideline.description: Comprehensive debugging workflow: 1. Use New Relic to identify errors and performance issues 2. Use Browser Tools to check console errors and network issues 3. Use PostgreSQL MCP for LOCAL database query analysis and debugging 4. Use Supabase MCP get_logs to inspect REMOTE Edge Function behavior 5. Use Cloudflare observability for Worker diagnostics 6. Test fixes locally with PostgreSQL MCP before deploying via Supabase MCP

reference_usage.instruction: To reference these tools in prompts, include specific tool names and their primary use cases relevant to the SlideHeroes development context.
reference_usage.example_prompt: You have access to the following MCP tools for SlideHeroes development: - New Relic MCP for monitoring and debugging - PostgreSQL MCP for database operations - Supabase MCP for platform management - GitHub MCP for version control - Context7 MCP for documentation - Browser Tools for testing Use these tools as documented in .claude/build/5-tools/available-tools.xml
